mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.08% indicates deep accommodation regime
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rates near zero
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate profit expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 148,319 thousand suggests near-threshold employment level
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained job creation
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 58% of instances when PAYEMS growth exceeds 150,000 persons, or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces earnings expectations and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 3% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 625,416M and HOUST at 1,555K units show recent stability
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint decline) shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (interaction coefficient p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production below 99.5 index points signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, constraining profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 99.5 and remains there for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 100.0178 is near recent peak levels
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate above 5.0% combined with stagnant payroll growth signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.0% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth falls below 50,000 persons
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.6% and PAYEMS at 148,319K show labor market still resilient
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase preceding recession
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 62% of triggered instances, or the joint condition shows no incremental predictive power beyond UNRATE alone (interaction p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the Consumer Price Index above 0.5% signals accelerating inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain or raise rates, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 276.724 reflects recent inflation dynamics
regime_hint: inflationary pressure regime with policy tightening risk
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP (GDPC1) below 0.5% growth signals economic deceleration that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days in the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate falls below 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 19,465.195B reflects recent economic activity
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown or early recession phase
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) above 1,600 thousand units and retail sales (RSAFS) growth above 0.8% month-over-month signals robust consumer and residential demand that sustains corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1,600 thousand AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.8%
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1,555K and RSAFS at 625,416M show demand near trigger thresholds
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized demand strength
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint demand signal shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (interaction coefficient p > 0.10).
